items.title: Chinese APT Abuses Multiple Cloud Tools to Spy on Mongolia
items.summary: The threat actor gave itself plenty of options to support command and control, tapping Microsoft Outlook, Slack, Discord, and file.io for online espionage.

items.title: Hackers exploit file upload bug in Breeze Cache WordPress plugin
items.summary: Hackers are actively exploiting a critical vulnerability in the Breeze Cache plugin for WordPress that allows uploading arbitrary files on the server without authentication. [...]

items.title: China-Backed Hackers Are Industrializing Botnets
items.summary: China's state-backed groups are now using covert networks of compromised devices to execute attacks in a low-cost, low-risk, and deniable way.

items.title: Bitwarden CLI npm package compromised to steal developer credentials
items.summary: The Bitwarden CLI was briefly compromised after attackers uploaded a malicious @bitwarden/cli package to npm containing a credential-stealing payload capable of spreading to other projects. [...]

items.title: Bad Memories Still Haunt AI Agents
items.summary: Cisco found and fixed a significant vulnerability in the way Anthropic handles memories, but experts warn that mishandled memory files will continue threaten AI systems.

items.title: Regular Password Resets Aren’t as Safe as You Think
items.summary: Password resets are one of the easiest ways for attackers to bypass security controls. Specops Software shows how helpdesk social engineering turns a seemingly legitimate reset request into full account compromise. [...]

items.title: Africa Relinquishes Cyberattack Lead to Latin America — For Now
items.summary: The volume of cyberattacks targeting Africa declined in the past year, with weekly attacks down 22%, as attackers seemingly shifted their focus to other regions.
